title: Tech Disruptors: New Commercial Wave in Offensive Cyber Warfare
summary: “For commercial companies trying to operate in this space, they have to be willing to understand that what they’re building is fundamentally different, and if they’re not willing to invest in this way, they can struggle with adoption,” said Skyler Onken, co-founder of Twenty, an offensive-cyber company seeking to reshape cyber warfare. On this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast, Onken joins Bloomberg Intelligence senior defense weapons analyst Wayne Sanders to discuss the speed, scaling and

title: Vanguards of Health Care: Cortica’s Whole-Child Autism Bet
summary: “It’s crazy to think that that would be the expectation in cancer care, yet we expect that of families in autism,” says Neil Hattangadi, CEO and co-founder of Cortica. He joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Jonathan Palmer to explain why autism care remains so fragmented and how Cortica is trying to replace siloed services with a coordinated whole-child model. They discuss why applied behavior analysis (ABA) should be one tool, not the whole answer, how Cortica integrates medical and behavioral

title: StanChart Sells Some Card Business to India Lender in Pullback
summary: Standard Chartered Bank Plc has agreed to sell part of its Indian credit card business to Blackstone-backed Federal Bank Ltd., joining a slew of US and European lenders exiting the crowded retail segment dominated by local players.

title: Shapoorji Unit Markets High-Yield Debt to JPMorgan, BlackRock
summary: India’s Shapoorji and Pallonji Group is in talks with global investors including BlackRock and JPMorgan Chase & Co. for a high-yield bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, even after it was allowed to delay repayment on existing debt.

title: Energy Strategy in an Era of Global Fragmentation (Podcast)
summary: Middle powers are navigating a more fragmented global order. As tensions rise between major economies, the countries caught in between are being forced to rethink how they manage trade, security and economic ties. Energy is at the center of that shift. Reducing reliance on imported fuels is becoming a strategic priority, with electrification and domestic clean energy seen not just as climate solutions, but as tools of resilience and sovereignty. title: Unilever India Flags War-Led Volatility Even as Profit Beats
summary: Hindustan Unilever Ltd. flagged that Iran-war-driven volatility raises the risk of fresh cost pressures and supply disruptions, even as profit beat expectations on the back of tax-cut-enabled price reductions.

title: ECB Keeps Rates Steady With War’s Hit to Economy Still Unfolding
summary: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged, with officials signaling they need more time to assess the extent of the Iran war’s jolt to the economy.

title: ECB Keeps Interest Rates Unchanged
summary: The European Central Bank headquarters in Frankfurt. Photographer: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV/AFP

title: Bailey: BOE Sees Inflation Little Over 3.5% by Year End
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said inflation is expected to rise to “a little over 3.5% by the end of the year” as a direct consequence of the conflict in the Middle East. He was speaking during a press conference following the central bank’s decision to keep interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes.

title: Macron Moves to Name Top Ally Moulin to Lead Bank of France
summary: Emmanuel Macron’s chief of staff will leave next week, clearing a path for the French president to nominate his close ally to run the central bank.

title: UK Two-Year Bonds Extend Gains as BOE Governor Bailey Speaks
summary: UK bonds extended a rally as traders pared bets on Bank of England interest-rate hikes, after officials left policy unchanged and Governor Andrew Bailey it was in a “reasonable place.”

title: Bank of England Holds Rates as Officials Consider Hikes Ahead
summary: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes, just as oil prices soared within reach of the central bank’s most pessimistic scenario for the economy.

title: US Equity Futures Rise as Mega-Cap Earnings Lift Tech, Oil Drops
summary: US stock futures advanced on Thursday after some mega-cap and other tech companies reported bullish earnings lifting their stocks, while oil prices fell from a four-year high.

title: US Premarket Movers for April 30, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.4% as of 7:48 a.m. in New York after a blockbuster slate of technology earnings.

title: US Futures Gain on Boost From Earnings; Yen Jumps: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose as investors took positives from a deluge of earnings, including four of the Magnificent Seven tech giants. The yen rallied sharply after Japanese officials stepped up verbal support for the currency.

title: Emerging Stocks Set for Best Month Since 2009 Despite Oil Fear
summary: Emerging equities were in the red on the last trading day of April after oil prices surged above $125 per barrel, though they stayed on track for their best month since 2009.

title: OPEC+ Delegates Expect Another Symbolic Supply Hike Without UAE
summary: OPEC+ is likely to agree another symbolic production increase for June, in the group’s first move since the surprise departure of the United Arab Emirates, three delegates said.

title: India’s Rupee Trims Losses After Record Low as Oil Rally Fades
summary: India’s rupee slid to a record low on Thursday as a surge in crude prices rattled markets, before paring most of its losses after oil retreated from a four-year high.

title: Meta Kicks Off Bond Offering After Boosting Spending Outlook
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is tapping the investment-grade bond market again, six months after raising $30 billion in one of the market’s biggest-ever corporate debt deals, as the Facebook parent boosts spending on data centers and artificial intelligence.

title: European Power Plunges to Record Negative Prices on Solar Surge
summary: European power prices slid to record lows for Friday — being set at huge negative rates in Germany and France — as strong solar generation coincides with public holidays across much of the region.

title: Eric Trump Disappears From Troubled Fintech’s Public Leadership
summary: Eric Trump has disappeared from the public leadership of a small Las Vegas-based company closely tied to his family’s crypto firm, World Liberty Financial Inc.

title: Oil Drops From Four-Year High as War Fear Vies With Economic Hit
summary: Brent oil pulled back from a four-year high as concerns of escalating conflict in the Middle East jostled with signs of the economic toll the war is taking.

title: Ukraine Strikes Key Russian Refinery, Pumping Station in Perm
summary: Ukrainian drones struck a major refinery deep inside Russia and again attacked an oil-pumping station nearby, further crippling Moscow’s crude-processing capability.

title: Prada Sales Meet Views as War Slows Growth at Miu Miu Brand
summary: Prada SpA reported first quarter sales that met expectations, weathering war in the Middle East that dragged on sales of the fashion group’s popular Miu Miu line.

title: Veja os Três Assuntos Que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
summary:
O repórter Daniel Carvalho conta quais foram os três assuntos que 
marcaram a semana no Brasil. Com produção de Barbara Nascimento e edição de Raphael Almeida (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOE Holds Rates as Officials Signal Future Hikes
summary: The Bank of England officials voted 8-1 in favor of keeping the benchmark interest rate on hold at 3.75%. Chief Economist Huw Pill was the sole dissenting voice on the panel, but other officials on the Monetary Policy Committee signaled they could join him at upcoming meetings. Bloomberg's Lizzy Burden reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Royal Caribbean Shares Jump as Outlook Suggests Resilient Demand
summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. shares rose as the company’s outlook for the year suggests it will be able to weather headwinds such as higher fuel costs and disruptions on its Middle Eastern itineraries.

title: Chinese Demand Destruction Is Key to Calming Global LNG Prices
summary: Years of planning helped Beijing to cut imports, freeing up more fuel for other markets.

title: Oil Hits Wartime High; Tech Earnings; ECB, BOE Rate Decisions | Bloomberg Brief 4/30/2026
summary: US equity futures waver and oil fluctuates after hitting a wartime high following a report that President Trump is set to receive a briefing on new military options for action in Iran. Mixed results in tech earnings show Alphabet and Amazon's payoff in AI spending, while Meta lagged behind. Investors await the latest PCE data and rate decisions from the Bank of England and the European Central Bank. Jordan Rochester of Mizuho Bank anticipates more split votes under Kevin Warsh as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Angelo Zino of CFRA discusses tech earnings.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan's 'Final Warning' on Yen as US Rate Gap, Iran Tensions Bite | The Pulse 4/30
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Sören Radde, Point72, Head of European Economic Research; Iain Stealey, JPMorgan Asset Management, Fixed Income International CIO; Nive Bhagat, Capgemini CFO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Polish Rate Cut Chances Are Quickly Evaporating, Dabrowski Says
summary: Polish interest-rate cut chances significantly diminished after inflation accelerated in April, according to policymaker Ireneusz Dabrowski.

title: Blue Owl Asset Growth Meets Estimates Amid Direct Lending Slump
summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s fee-related earnings and assets increased as the alternative investment firm leaned on other parts of its business amid souring sentiment toward private credit.

title: Hedge Fund Manager ‘Ignoring Trump’ Nets 39% Gain on Energy Bets
summary: A hedge fund manager focused on navigating the energy transition has generated a 39% return so far this year, after adopting a policy of ignoring US President Donald Trump’s signals on the Iran war.

title: SpaceX Investor 137 Raises $700 Million Ahead of Musk-Led IPO
summary: 137 Ventures, a repeat investor in Elon Musk’s SpaceX, has raised $700 million across two new funds to back startups over longer time horizons.

title: Unilever Expects to Raise Detergent Prices on Iran War Costs
summary: Unilever Plc will likely increase prices for cleaning sprays and laundry detergents in emerging markets due to costs linked to the war in Iran.

title: Europe’s Banks Earmark $710 Million to Brace for Iran War Impact
summary: Europe’s top banks have set aside hundreds of millions of euros to shield against a financial impact from the Iran war, highlighting how the economic risks for the region are on the rise.
